Using Like Bases to Solve Exponential Equations

Inotherwords,whenanexponentialequationhasthesamebaseoneachside,theexponentsmustbeequal.Thisalso applies whentheexponentsarealebraicexpression.Thereforewecansolvemanyexponentialqutionbusingtherulesofexponents to rewriteeachsideasapowerwiththesamebase.Then,weusethefactthatexponentialfunctionsareone-to-onetosetthe exponents equal to one another,and solve for the unknown.

For example, considerthe equation $3^{4x-7}=\frac{3^{2x}}{3}$ .To solve for x,we use the division propertyof exponents to rewrite theright side sothatbothsideshavethecommonbase3.Thenweapplytheone-to-onepropertofexponentbseintheexponents equal to one another and solving for x:

$\begin{aligned}3^{4x-7}&=\frac{3^{2x}}{3}\\3^{4x-7}&=\frac{3^{2x}}{3^{1}}\\3^{4x-7}&=3^{2x-1}\\4x-7&=2x-1\\2x&=6\\x&=3\end{aligned}$ Divide by 3Rewrite 3 as Subtract2x s)$3^{1}$ n propertyofexponents dadd7tobothsides xponents
